column1: GLOBE Observer Connect: Bringing Your Observations to Meteorologists
column20: Join the representatives from GLOBE Program Office, NOAA, and Synoptic Data to learn how they are bringing your citizen science observations to meteorologists.
column21: Please join us on 19 March 2026, at 12:00 pm ET (16:00 UTC) to learn how GLOBE data benefits the meteorological community. In March 2025, environmental data collected by the GLOBE community became available in NOAA‚Äôs Meteorological Assimilation Data Ingest System (MADIS). Supported by Synoptic Data, this integration helps bridge the gap between citizen science and professional meteorological analysis. By making GLOBE observations available via MADIS and the Synoptic Data Viewer, the GLOBE community is contributing data to help scientists understand Earth‚Äôs complex systems. Hear directly from the architects of this collaboration and join us for a conversation with staff from the GLOBE Program Office, NOAA, and Synoptic Data, who will share insights into this exciting milestone, describe examples of how the data are used, and demonstrate how the data in MADIS can be accessed.
column32: GLOBE, citizen science, atmosphere, clouds, meteorology

column1: GLOBE Observer Connect: Blowing Snow Results
column20: Join NOAA research scientist Bill Line and GLOBE Implementation Office's Christina Buffington for a conversation about they are using your observations of blowing snow.
column21: From December 2025 through March 2026, GLOBE invited the public to participate in a new blowing snow pilot project. This GLOBE Observer Connect conversation with Bill Line (NOAA NESDIS) and Christina Buffington (GIO and University of Alaska Fairbanks) will share the results from the pilot project. The purpose of the blowing snow project is to fill a gap where ground-based photos are needed to validate a blowing snow satellite product made by National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) National Environmental Satellite, Data, and Information Service (NESDIS). We asked the GLOBE community to collect feature photos across the horizon that showed snow being lifted from the ground tens to hundreds of meters into the sky by winds. This Connect conversation will show how the blowing snow satellite product works and how GLOBE observations can continue to support it.
column32: GLOBE, citizen science, atmosphere, clouds, meteorology, snow
